domain: Maori tā moko (chiselled facial tattoo; unique per individual; encodes genealogy + life events + status; permanent and tamper-evident; used as legal signature; multi-attribute readout)
specific_mechanism: Multi-attribute permanent identity mark carved into substrate; each attribute encoded in a different facial region; tampering visibly destroys the corresponding region.
llm_application: MOKO-FINGERPRINT: a multi-attribute LLM fingerprint. For attributes A = {model_id, owner_id, version, training_date}, choose disjoint coordinate-sets Π_a in parameter space. Apply small fingerprint perturbations δ_a at Π_a encoding attribute a. Verify attribute a via attribute-specific probe Q_a → response statistical test r_a. Each attribute independently readable; tampering with Π_a destroys r_a but leaves other attributes intact (graceful degradation). Differs from single-bit fingerprints (Markov Chain Lock, EditMF, Chain & Hash) by ORTHOGONAL MULTI-ATTRIBUTE encoding with PARTIAL READOUT survivability.
novelty_claim: Semantically Conditioned Watermarks (2505.16723) is single statistical signal. Chain & Hash, Markov Chain Lock, EditMF, ImF are single-payload. Instructional Fingerprinting embeds one instruction-trigger pair. MOKO-FINGERPRINT differs by (a) MULTIPLE ORTHOGONAL ATTRIBUTES encoded at DISJOINT coordinate sets, (b) PARTIAL ATTRIBUTE READOUT (one tampered region doesn't destroy others), (c) ATTRIBUTE-SPECIFIC PROBE QUERIES.
